method_atom: Analysis of a localized inhomogeneity — a lower-dimensional (point, line, or plane) defect — that couples quadratically to a fluctuating order-parameter field of a many-body system tuned to a continuous critical point at or above its upper critical dimension. Such a coupling nucleates a localized region of order around the defect whose core size is set by the strength of the local perturbation and which develops a universal, slowly (power-law) decaying tail. The tail opens many low-energy dissipative channels that couple to the nucleated collective degree of freedom and completely suppress its coherent tunneling dynamics.
object_atom: A metallic electron system near a magnetic quantum critical point containing a single non-magnetic impurity or extended defect, around which a spin droplet forms; the resulting impurity-induced change in the local spin susceptibility is proposed to be measured by an NMR experiment.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is a localized defect coupling to the square of an order parameter at criticality that nucleates a core-plus-universal-tail structure whose dissipative coupling quenches tunneling; the domain-specific object is the metallic spin system, its impurity, and the NMR probe.
